title: fix: Return input instead of panicking if empty subset in `drop_nulls()` and `drop_nans()`
author: etiennebacher
body:
Fixes #22605

Listed here: https://gist.github.com/devdanzin/dc2ef895928fc5758ea66a1ccf2eedd2

Ping @devdanzin

---

**Before:**

```python
import polars as pl
pl.DataFrame({"a": [1.0, float("nan")]}).drop_nans([])
```
```
pyo3_runtime.PanicException: called `Result::unwrap()` on an `Err` value: ComputeError(ErrString("cannot return empty fold because the number of output rows is unknown"))
```

**After:**

```python
import polars as pl
pl.DataFrame({"a": [1.0, float("nan")]}).drop_nans([])
```
```
shape: (2, 1)
┌─────┐
│ a   │
│ --- │
│ f64 │
╞═════╡
│ 1.0 │
│ NaN │
└─────┘
```

comments.author: etiennebacher
comments.body: My bad, I guess `drop_nans([])` should be the same as `drop_nans()`.

comments.author: etiennebacher
comments.body: Ok, I'll revert to the behavior in the first commit

comments.author: orlp
comments.body:
To justify my above request, note the description of `drop_nulls`:

> Drop all rows that contain null values.

and `drop_nans`:

> Drop all rows that contain one or more NaN values.

If the subset of columns considered is empty neither condition is ever met, so no rows should be dropped.
